name: Conform (Progressive Enhancement Form Validation)

performance.notes: Near-zero overhead; progressive enhancement means no JS required for basic submission

agent_readiness.notes: Progressive enhancement form library for React with server-side validation. useForm(), useInputControl(). Works with server actions (Next.js, Remix, SvelteKit). Zod, Yup, Valibot schema integration via parseWithZod(). Field metadata via getInputProps(), getSelectProps(). Nested objects and arrays. Error handling from server actions. TypeScript-first. No controlled components by default — minimal re-renders. The recommended form library for Remix and Next.js Server Actions workflows.

gotchas: Less ergonomic for purely client-side validation vs react-hook-form
Server action integration may be overkill for simple forms
Newer library — smaller community than react-hook-form
best_when: Remix or Next.js 14+ Server Actions forms
Forms needing progressive enhancement (work without JS)
Full-stack TypeScript apps with Zod schema validation
When server-side validation errors need to flow back to form fields
avoid_when: Legacy React without server action support
summary: Conform is a progressive enhancement form library optimized for Remix and Next.js Server Actions. useForm() with Zod/Yup/Valibot schema integration via parseWithZod(). Server-side validation errors flow to form fields automatically. TypeScript-first, minimal re-renders. The recommended form library for full-stack TypeScript apps using Server Actions. react-hook-form is better for client-only forms.
